svg.title: HyperWeave — Where Vectors Meet Vision
svg.desc: HyperWeave stacked logo, Primer porcelain variant (fired white glaze, cobalt underglaze — the editorial default). Octahedral mark with faceted volume, glass core, and a lit-to-shadow transmitter highlight, above the HyperWeave wordmark in Orbitron over WHERE VECTORS MEET VISION / INNERAURA LABS. Transparent (light surfaces).

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: HyperWeave stacked lockup, Primer porcelain colorway — fired white glaze, cobalt underglaze — the editorial default.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Identity and motion held constant; a depth pass adds a faceted octahedron volume (lit top to shadow bottom via a userSpace vertical gradient over the four faces), a translucent glass core, a soft back-glow plane, and a transmitter highlight that runs white at the crown to shadow at the base — giving the wireframe dimensional layering instead of a flat plane. Palette swapped to the Primer porcelain tokens.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Earlier mark read flat and rigid; added translucent volume/glass/glow LAYERS for depth while keeping the crisp octahedral geometry rather than rounding it, so the precision identity holds. Light variants stay on a transparent field; dark variants carry their flat surface_0 since their bright accents require a dark ground to read. Fills derive from the variant accent so they stay tonally unified, never the muddy multi-hue wash of the prior flux faces.
hw:reasoning.hw:spatial-notes: 604x676 (1.12:1), ~36px balanced margins. Axis of symmetry x=302. Octahedral complex centered (302,238) at scale 2.42; vertices r=80 local; faceted faces shaded on a vertical light(-80)->dark(+80) gradient for volume; transmitter glass fill -60..60; inner highlight crown-to-base on a 0.93-scaled concentric path. Five connection nodes (4 vertices r=3.5 + center r=4). Wordmark cap 52px baseline 562; subtitle slots 606/640.
